WIND DAMAGE CONCENTRATED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CARLTON RD AND HIGHWAY 133 WAS DETERMINED TO BE CAUSED BY A MICROBURST. DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENTLY BLOWN TO THE NORTHEAST. IN ADDITION TO SOME TREE DAMAGE... A COUPLE HOMES HAD DAMAGE TO SIDING...SHINGLES...PORCH OVERHANGS AND OUTBUILDINGS/SHEDS. MOST DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH 65-70 MPH GUSTS. IRRIGATION PIVOT FLIPPED ALONG ISABELLA-NASHVILLE ROAD TO THE SOUTHWEST OF THE BEGINNING OF A TORNADO. MORE CONCENTRATED TREE DAMAGE JUST EAST ALONG SALEM CHURCH RD AS WELL...INDICATING A DOWNBURST.

BRIEF EF0 TORNADO ON THE WESTERN OUTSKIRTS OF TIFTON... JUST TO THE SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 82. PATH LENGTH APPROX 2.3 MILES. PATH WIDTH APPROX 300 YARDS. PEAK GUSTS AROUND 80-85 MPH. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS TREE DAMAGE...BUT SOME STRUCTURES WERE AFFECTED. A MANUFACTURED HOME SITTING ON BLOCKS WAS BLOWN AND SLID 20 FEET. A METAL BUILDING UNDER CONSTRUCTION PARTIALLY COLLAPSED. TWO SHEDS WERE FLIPPED. AND TWO LARGE RETAIL STORES HAD SOME MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND AC UNITS MOVED OR TIPPED.

TREE DAMAGE IN THE CITY OF TIFTON. A COUPLE TREES BLOWN DOWN ON VEHICLES OR RESIDENCES. METAL ROOFING OFF A BUILDING ALONG BEECH STREET. A COUPLE STREET SIGNS BLOWN DOWN. GUSTS LIKELY PEAKED IN THE 65-75 MPH RANGE.

A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN IRWIN COUNTY SOUTH OF OCILLA GA AND WAS AT ITS STRONGEST NEAR THE END OF ITS 2 MILES DAMAGE PATH. EF1 DAMAGE WAS ASSESSED. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS ALSO NOTED IN THIS AREA. A MOBILE HOME WAS KNOCKED SEVERAL FEET OFF ITS FOUNDATION, BUT REMAINED UPRIGHT. MOST OF THE ROOF WAS TORN OFF. A LARGE HARDWOOD TREE WAS SNAPPED IN HALF AND MANY OTHERS WERE UPROOTED. SEVERAL OUT BUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED AND SHINGLES WERE STRIPPED FROM THE ROOF OF ONE HOME.

THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN A HALF MILE OR SO EAST OFHIGHWAY 77 SOUTH OF GRACEVILLE. DAMAGE WAS MOSTLY TO TREES. HOWEVER...SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED. ONE WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED WHEN A LARGE OAK TREE WAS TOPPLED ONTO IT. DAMAGE TO TREES...AND TO ONE OF THE HOMES EAST OF HIGHWAY 77...WAS CONSISTENT WITH EF1 DAMAGE.
ONE HOUSE COMPLETELY DESTORYED AT THE INTERSECTION OF 77 AND TRI COUNTY ROAD. ANOTHER HOME ON TRI COUNTY ROAD WITH ROOF DAMAGE. CORRECTED TIME OF EVENT.

A SECOND TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED IN JACKSON COUNTY FL STARTING ALONG HIGHWAY 231 SOUTHEAST OF CAMPBELLTON WITH A FEW TREES BLOWN OVER AND SNAPPED. THERE WAS EVIDENCE OF SPORADIC DAMAGE TO TREES TO THE NORTH-NORTHEAST. THE SECOND AND MORE SIGNIFICANT AREA OF DAMAGE WAS TO A GROVE OF PINE TREES EAST OF CAMPBELLTON ALONG HIGHWAY 2. THERE WAS CLEAR EVIDENCE OF A TORNADO AT THIS LOCATION CONSISTENT WITH HIGHER-END EF1 DAMAGE. THERE WAS NO EVIDENCE OF DAMAGE VERY FAR TO THE NORTHEAST OF THIS ROAD.

POSSIBLE TORNADO. EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS STRUCTURES DAMAGED AT LANGDALE FOREST PRODUCTS WITH ONE BUILDING COLLAPSED. RAILROAD CARS OVERTURNED. OTHER STRUCTURES IN THE AREA HAVE ROOF DAMAGE.

*** 9 INJ *** DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMS A TORNADO AFFECTED THE SOUTH SIDE OF VALDOSTA. RATED MAXIMUM EF2. PATH LENGTH 0.8 MILES. MAX WIDTH 180 YARDS. MOST SEVERE DAMAGE WAS ON THE LANGDALE FOREST PRODUCTS PROPERTY WHERE SEVERAL METAL BUILDINGS WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. SOME VEHICLES WERE TOSSED AROUND 70 YARDS. TWO RAIL CARS WERE ALSO TIPPED OVER.
